root_text.sn47.29:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn47.29:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sirivaḍḍho gahapati ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn47.29:1.3: Atha kho sirivaḍḍho gahapati aññataraṁ purisaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn47.29:1.4: “ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vanda:
root_text.sn47.29:1.6: So āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vandatī’ti.
root_text.sn47.29:1.8: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, āyasmā ānando yena sirivaḍḍhassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn47.29:1.9: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so puriso sirivaḍḍhassa gahapatissa paṭissutvā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so puriso āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.29:1.10: “sirivaḍḍho, bhante, gahapati ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno, so āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vandati.
root_text.sn47.29:1.12: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, āyasmā ānando yena sirivaḍḍhassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn47.29:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena sirivaḍḍhassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho āyasmā ānando sirivaḍḍhaṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.29:2.2: “kacci te, gahapati, khamanīyaṁ kacci yāpanīyaṁ, kacci dukkhā vedanā paṭikkamanti, no abhikkamanti; paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.sn47.29:2.3: “Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo”ti.
root_text.sn47.29:3.2: ‘kāye kāyānupassī viharissāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.29:3.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharissāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassan’ti.
root_text.sn47.29:4.1: “Yeme, bhante, bhagavatā cattāro satipaṭṭhānā desitā saṁvijjanti, te dhammā mayi, ahañca tesu dhammesu sandissāmi.
root_text.sn47.29:4.2: Ahañhi, bhante, kāye kāyānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.29:4.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.29:4.6: Yāni cimāni, bhante, bhagavatā pañcorambhāgiyāni saṁyojanāni desitāni, nāhaṁ, bhante, tesaṁ kiñci attani appahīnaṁ samanupassāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.29:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn47.29:1.2: Now at that time the householder Sirivaḍḍha was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn47.29:1.4: “Please, my friend, go to Venerable Ānanda, and in my name bow with your head to his feet. Say to him:
translation_text.sn47.29:1.5: ‘Sir, the householder Sirivaḍḍha is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn47.29:1.6: He bows with his head to your feet.’
translation_text.sn47.29:1.8: ‘Sir, please visit him at his home out of sympathy.’”
translation_text.sn47.29:1.9: “Yes, sir,” that man replied. He did as Sirivaḍḍha asked.
translation_text.sn47.29:2.1: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the householder Sirivaḍḍha, sat down on the seat spread out, and said to him:
translation_text.sn47.29:2.2: “I hope you’re keeping well, householder; I hope you’re all right. And I hope the pain is fading, not growing, that its fading is evident, not its growing.”
translation_text.sn47.29:2.3: “Sir, I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. The pain is terrible and growing, not fading; its growing is evident, not its fading.”
translation_text.sn47.29:3.2: ‘I’ll meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.29:3.3: I’ll meditate on an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.29:3.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.’
translation_text.sn47.29:4.1: “These four kinds of mindfulness meditation that were taught by the Buddha are found in me, and I exhibit them.
translation_text.sn47.29:4.2: For I meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.29:4.3: I meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.29:4.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.29:4.6: And of the five lower fetters taught by the Buddha, I don’t see any that I haven’t given up.”
translation_text.sn47.29:4.8: You have declared the fruit of non-return.”
